headline: Large Energy User Grid Cost Shifting
short_summary: This bill aims to shift electricity costs from residential customers to large energy users like data centers, while also extending energy assistance and weatherization programs for vulnerable individuals.
status: Last Action: Governor's recommendation received by House on 2026-04-13
supporters: Residential customers, advocates for low-income/elderly/disabled individuals, Dominion Energy (initially), Senator L. Louise Lucas, Delegate LeVere Bolling.
opponents: Data centers, large industrial users, critics of the Governor's amendments.
news_headline: Governor's amendments to energy and data center legislation
news_summary: Governor Abigail Spanberger proposed amendments to bills, including HB 1393, that aim to shift electricity costs from residential customers to data centers and large industrial users. While supporters claim these shifts could save residents money, critics argue the amendments might weaken the original intent of the legislation.

headline: Data Center Water Consumption Reporting
short_summary: This bill mandates that water users supplying data centers report their monthly or quarterly water consumption, including reclaimed water, to the State Water Control Board for transparency and resource management.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 896 (Effective 1/1/2027) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Environmental groups (e.g., Potomac Conservancy, Piedmont Environmental Council), local communities, water authorities, Senator Kannan Srinivasan.
opponents: Data center industry (due to reporting burden or potential future restrictions).
news_headline: Potomac River Most Endangered, Citing Data Center Water Demands
news_summary: The Potomac River was designated the most endangered U.S. river in 2026 due to rapid data center expansion and water demands. SB 553, which requires utilities to report water volumes supplied to data centers, is seen as a crucial first step towards transparency, though advocates desire more detailed reporting.

headline: Data Center Siting and Noise Limits
short_summary: This bill requires additional local review for new high-energy-use data centers, including site assessments for noise, water, agricultural, and historical impacts before zoning approval.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 567 (effective 7/1/2026) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Local residents, environmental groups (e.g., Piedmont Environmental Council), local governments, Delegate Josh Thomas.
opponents: Data center developers and industry groups.
news_headline: Sterling residents worry about data center noise, health
news_summary: Residents in Sterling are experiencing constant noise from a data center, raising concerns about quality of life and potential health impacts from emissions. Loudoun County officials are discussing mitigation efforts and reviewing noise ordinances in response to growing complaints.
